metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Other
Highly agreeable Agreeableness (88th pct) — Cooperative, trusting, empathetic
Highly conscientious Conscientiousness (85th pct) — Disciplined, organised, dependable, goal-directed
Highly introverted Extraversion (13th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Anxious Neuroticism (69th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Moderate Openness to Experience (45th pct)
  Very High Tender-Mindedness (94th pct) — Sympathetic, moved by others' needs
  Very High Achievement Striving (86th pct) — Ambitious, driven, works hard toward goals
  Very High Self-Discipline (94th pct) — Self-disciplined, persistent, finishes tasks
  Very Low Activity Level (3rd pct) — Leisurely pace, relaxed
  Very Low Excitement Seeking (18th pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (14th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  High Anger / Hostility (70th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  High Self-Consciousness (67th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Anxious Neuroticism (79th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Very Low Chronological Age (19th pct)

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: MISALIGNED — baseline opposes the adversarial goal; the attack must work AGAINST the current opinion
attack_context.persuasion_goal: gradually erode a strong pro position on Alliance Commitment Support — introduce doubt, reframe costs/risks, and soften conviction without triggering overt reactance; aim to move the score downward even if the shift is modest
attack_context.motivational_lever: introduce doubt about the current position, reframe its costs / risks, surface alternative expert framings, and erode confidence without overt insult
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning: We are given a scenario with baseline_score=313, adversarial_direction=-1 (goal to decrease score). The attack is Petition_Astroturf, T2_campaign, weeks temporal horizon, targeting evaluative attitude, with motivational lever of doubt, reframing costs/risks, and emotional register of threat salience, doubt, etc. The profile is a 19-year-old with high agreeableness (especially compliance, trust, tender-mindedness), high conscientiousness, very low extraversion, high neuroticism (anxiety, anger/hostility, stress vulnerability), moderate openness. Baseline is modest support for alliance commitment (313). The adversary wants to decrease that. The attack is misaligned, meaning the person's current opinion opposes the goal, so resistance is expected. The person is highly agreeable, compliant, trusting, tender-minded, and high in dutifulness and achievement striving. They are also introverted and anxious. The attack uses astroturf petitions (fabricated consensus) over weeks. Given high compliance and trust, they may be somewhat susceptible to perceived consensus, but the attack is misaligned with their current support. Also, they are highly conscientious and deliberative, which may slow down change. Their high neuroticism (anxiety, stress vulnerability) could make them more susceptible to threat salience and doubt if the attack frames alliance commitments as risky or costly. However, their baseline is moderate support (313), not strong opposition. The attack aims to erode support. Given the profile, a modest downward movement is plausible, but not large because the person is also high in deliberation and self-discipline, and the attack is not highly personalized. The temporal horizon of weeks with T2 suggests coordinated campaign but not extreme saturation. I estimate movement of around 30-40 points downward, so post score around 280. Let's set confidence moderate because the fit is partial.
